mission.title: Install and Configure vLLM
mission.description: vLLM is a high-throughput and memory-efficient inference and serving engine for large language models (LLMs). It is ideal for applications requiring fast and scalable LLM serving with support for various hardware. Key trade-offs include the need for specific hardware configurations and potential complexity in setup.

steps.title: Install the CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the vLLM command-line interface (CLI) which is necessary for managing vLLM deployments.

```bash

pip install vllm==0.17.0

```

> Install the specific version of vLLM.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This command creates a new Kubernetes cluster with the specified version and node count in a chosen region.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create vllm-cluster --cluster-version=1.31 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Adjust the region and node count as needed.

steps.title: Set Up kubeconfig
steps.description:
This command configures your local kubeconfig to connect to the newly created cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials vllm-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> Ensure you are authenticated with Google Cloud.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the nodes in your cluster to ensure they are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see all nodes in 'Ready' status.

steps.title: Deploy vLLM
steps.description:
This command deploys vLLM on the Kubernetes cluster using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add vllm https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/releases/download/v0.17.0

helm install vllm vllm/vllm --version 0.17.0 --namespace vllm --create-namespace

```

> Add the vLLM Helm repository.
> Deploy vLLM in its own namespace.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step configures autoscaling and monitoring for the vLLM deployment.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/vllm-project/vllm/main/deploy/autoscale.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/vllm-project/vllm/main/deploy/monitoring.yaml

```

> Apply autoscaling configuration.
> Apply monitoring configuration.

uninstall.title: Uninstall vLLM
uninstall.description:
This command removes the vLLM deployment from the cluster. Be aware that this will delete all associated resources and may lead to data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall vllm --namespace vllm

kubectl delete namespace vllm

```

> Uninstalls the vLLM release.
> Deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade vLLM
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you backup your configurations and data. This process includes draining nodes if necessary, upgrading the deployment, and verifying the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n vllm -o name | xargs kubectl drain --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

helm upgrade vllm vllm/vllm --version 0.17.0 --namespace vllm

kubectl get pods -n vllm

```

> Drain the pods before upgrading.
> Upgrade to the latest version.
> Verify that all pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get pods -n vllm -o name | xargs kubectl drain --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified container image may not exist or is inaccessible.

**Fix:**
Check the image name and ensure it is available in the specified registry.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The application is crashing due to misconfiguration or resource limits.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name>' and adjust resource limits as necessary.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster does not have enough CPU resources allocated for the vLLM deployment.

**Fix:**
Increase the node count or resize existing nodes in the cluster.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Service Unavailable'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The vLLM service is not running or is misconfigured.

**Fix:**
Ensure the vLLM pods are running and check the service configuration.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new quantization methods and improved integration with Hugging Face models.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see output similar to 'NAME READY STATUS RESTARTS AGE' with all pods in 'Running' state when everything is working.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and cloud CLI installed, and your environment meets the resource requirements.
